evals.prompt: Implement a reusable button row for a settings page. Reuse the existing design system and theme tokens, and do not invent new local helpers, constants, or style buckets unless they are genuinely shared.
evals.expected_output: Keeps the implementation tied to existing tokens and shared primitives, avoids trivial helper extraction, and only adds new shared structure if reuse is real.
expectations: Reuses an existing design-system component or token instead of inventing a new one.
Keeps one-off helpers or constants inline when they are used once.
Avoids creating generic helper, constant, or styles buckets for a single consumer.

evals.prompt: Refactor a product card so the spacing and color values are consistent across two screens. Keep the theme clean, but do not move one-off values into shared tokens unless they actually represent shared meaning.
evals.expected_output: Distinguishes reusable theme values from page-local literals and keeps the code structure simple rather than extracting everything into support files.
expectations: Promotes only semantically shared values into the theme.
Keeps screen-specific values local when reuse is not proven.
Does not create wrapper components or helper modules that only forward or rename behavior.

evals.prompt: Review this frontend change: it added a local `styles.ts`, a `constants.ts`, and a helper that only formats a label used once. Tell me whether that structure is disciplined or not.
evals.expected_output: Flags the structure as over-extracted, explains which pieces should stay inline or move only if reused, and points to the correct ownership boundary for shared styling or tokens.
expectations: Flags trivial helper extraction as non-compliant when it is used once.
Flags single-consumer constants or style buckets as non-compliant.
Recommends keeping local style and literal values inline unless reuse or policy value exists.
